specialties.name: Air Traffic Controller
specialties.what_it_is: NOT AN ENTRY-LEVEL MOS. NAVMC 1200.1M requires you to already be a qualified MOS 7257 or 7291. You reach 7252 from inside the air traffic control field — the entry point is the ATC trainee pipeline (7251 → 7257), and 7252 is awarded when you qualify for and are issued the FAA Control Tower Credential.

civilian_crosswalk.salary_source: DoD COOL / O*NET published median annual wage for the matched civilian occupations. This is the CIVILIAN wage, not military pay.
civilian_crosswalk.credential_gap_note: DoD COOL maps this job to 1 civilian occupation: Air Traffic Controllers. COOL lists no civilian credential this job maps to. Assume you leave with experience and no certificate, and that an employer will want the certificate.

specialties.name: Air Traffic Controller
specialties.what_it_is: The entry route is MOS 7251 (ATC trainee): you must meet the 7251 prerequisites and complete the Air Traffic Control Basic Course (N2367A2), then qualify on a live control position at an air station before the 7257 MOS is awarded — the manual allows up to 12 months after graduation. Lateral-move applicants must be a Cpl or below with less than 5 years in service.
sources: NAVMC 1200.1L, FY26 Marine Corps MOS Manual (official)

specialties.code: CODE STATUS: UNVERIFIED-CURRENT. Historically MOS 4341 Combat Correspondent — still referenced as the MOS awarded by DINFOS's Combat Correspondent Course per DINFOS's own training-department page (secondary-corroborated 2026-07-12). Administratively, the Marine Corps merged the legacy 43XX (Public Affairs) and 46XX (Combat Camera) fields into a new 45XX (Communication Strategy and Operations) occupational field per MARADMIN 484/17 (30 Aug 2017) and MARADMIN 534/17 (28 Sep 2017). A third-party MOS aggregator (mosroadmap.com) lists a '4531 Combat Mass Communicator' code within the 45XX field that may be 4341's current successor, but this was NOT confirmed against a primary Marine Corps MOS manual this session — presented with both the legacy designation and the known successor field, not guessed.

specialties.name: Intelligence Specialist
specialties.what_it_is: Under DoD's 13 Adjudicative Guidelines (Security Executive Agent Directive 4 / 32 CFR Part 147), none of the following are automatic mechanical disqualifiers under the 'whole person' standard, but they are the dominant real-world reasons applicants and incumbents are denied or lose eligibility, and each can rule out this specialty even with a fully qualifying ASVAB score: (1) unresolved foreign contacts, foreign relatives in certain countries, foreign property/financial interests, or foreign romantic ties that create potential foreign leverage (Guideline B, Foreign Influence); (2) a pattern of serious financial delinquency -- unresolved debt (especially over $10,000), recent bankruptcy, wage garnishment, or repeated late payment patterns (Guideline F; financial issues are cited as roughly 45% of DoD clearance denials); (3) illegal drug use, misuse of prescription medication, or continued use after applying (Guideline H); and (4), treated especially seriously, deliberately concealing or falsifying any of the above -- including an UNREPORTED dual citizenship or undisclosed foreign contact -- during the application or investigation (Guideline E, Personal Conduct). A 16-24-year-old considering this cluster should treat clearance eligibility as a real, independent gate, not a formality layered on top of a qualifying ASVAB score.

specialties.name: Tiltrotor Mechanic, MV-22
specialties.what_it_is: 6116 sits within the Marine Corps aviation occupational field (6000 series): the first two digits designate primary-position mechanic (60), helicopter mechanic (61), or fixed-wing mechanic (62) — per the official Marine Corps aircraft-maintenance careers page (marines.com, fetched 2026-07-12), which lists dozens of specific airframe MOSs in this field, including 6113 (Helicopter Mechanic, CH-53), 6116 (Tiltrotor Mechanic, MV-22), 6217/6218 (Fixed-Wing Aircraft Mechanic, F/A-18 and F-35), and the crew-chief/aircrew MOSs (617X series). 6116 was selected for this record as a genuinely distinctive, current, high-profile Marine Corps airframe (the MV-22B Osprey tiltrotor) rather than an arbitrary pick. A secondary source (UNVERIFIED) reports a five-year minimum enlistment term for this MOS.

entry_requirements.security_clearance: UNVERIFIED — not independently confirmed this session. Flightline/technical-manual access commonly requires at least a Secret clearance for equivalent aviation-maintenance MOSs in the other branches covered by this file.
